title: An old toy I still remember
summary: Describe an old toy and one short memory connected to it.
topic: An old toy I still remember
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about an old toy you still remember. Describe what the toy looked like and explain one short memory you have of it. Use past simple. Say how you felt about it then and how you feel now.
hintStarters: When I was young, I had a toy that I loved very much…
I remember the day I first got it because…
Even now, I still think about that toy when…
sampleText: When I was six years old, I had a small stuffed elephant that I carried everywhere. Its name was Bamba. It was grey and had big round ears and a short soft trunk. My aunt gave it to me on my birthday in Cape Town, and I took it home on the bus, holding it tightly on my lap. I played with Bamba every afternoon after school. One day, I left it on the playground and could not find it. I cried for a long time. My mother went back to the playground later that evening and found it near a bench. I was so happy when she walked through the door with it in her hand. After that, I kept Bamba on my bed and never took it outside again. Over the years, one of its eyes fell off and my grandmother sewed it back together with black thread. The toy became very worn out, but I did not care. I still have Bamba in a box at my parents' house in Cape Town. When I look at it now, I feel like that six-year-old boy again. It reminds me of a time when everything was simple and safe.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: big round ears and a short soft trunk
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tai to tròn và vòi ngắn mềm mại

sampleAnnotations.phrase: holding it tightly on my lap
I played with Bamba every afternoon
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi chơi với Bamba mỗi buổi chiều

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I left it on the playground
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi để nó trên sân chơi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I cried for a long time

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tìm thấy nó gần một chiếc ghế

sampleAnnotations.phrase: one of its eyes fell off
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một trong những mắt của nó rơi ra

sampleAnnotations.phrase: sewed it back together with black thread
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: khâu lại với nhau bằng chỉ đen

sampleAnnotations.phrase: It reminds me of a time when everything was simple
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Nó nhắc tôi nhớ đến thời mọi thứ còn đơn giản

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for completed actions and states
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past simple cho tất cả các hành động và trạng thái đã hoàn thành trong quá khứ: 'gave', 'took', 'cried', 'found'. Động từ bất quy tắc cần học thuộc (give → gave, take → took, find → found).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past simple for all completed actions and states in the past: 'gave', 'took', 'cried', 'found'. Irregular verbs must be memorised (give → gave, take → took, find → found).

sampleTranslationVi: Khi tôi sáu tuổi, tôi có một chú voi nhồi bông nhỏ mà tôi mang theo khắp nơi. Tên nó là Bamba. Nó màu xám, có đôi tai to tròn và chiếc vòi ngắn mềm mại. Cô tôi tặng nó cho tôi vào ngày sinh nhật ở Cape Town, và tôi đem nó về nhà bằng xe buýt, giữ chặt trên đùi. Tôi chơi với Bamba mỗi chiều sau khi tan học. Một ngày, tôi để quên nó trên sân chơi và không tìm thấy. Tôi khóc rất lâu. Mẹ tôi đã quay lại sân chơi tối hôm đó và tìm thấy nó gần một chiếc ghế băng. Tôi rất vui khi bà bước vào nhà với nó trên tay. Sau đó, tôi để Bamba trên giường và không bao giờ mang ra ngoài nữa. Theo năm tháng, một trong những mắt của nó rơi ra và bà tôi đã khâu lại bằng chỉ đen. Món đồ chơi trở nên rất cũ kỹ, nhưng tôi không quan tâm. Tôi vẫn còn giữ Bamba trong một chiếc hộp ở nhà bố mẹ tôi ở Cape Town. Khi nhìn vào nó bây giờ, tôi cảm thấy như cậu bé sáu tuổi ngày ấy. Nó nhắc tôi nhớ đến một thời khi mọi thứ còn đơn giản và bình yên.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple sentence about receiving a gift.
options: My aunt gave it to me on my birthday.
My aunt give it to me on my birthday.
My aunt gived it to me on my birthday.
My aunt was give it to me on my birthday.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Gave' is the irregular past simple of 'give'.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the irregular past form of 'give'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes a toy's appearance at A2 level?
options: It was grey and had big round ears and a short soft trunk.
It was grey and have big round ears and a short soft trunk.
It is grey and had big round ears and a short soft trunk.
It were grey and had big round ears and a short soft trunk.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'was' and 'had' are correct for describing the toy in the past.
mcQuestions.hint: Both verbs should be in the past tense.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'I ___ Bamba every afternoon after school.'
options: played with
play with
was play with
playing with
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'played with' is correct for a repeated past habit.
mcQuestions.hint: A repeated action in the past uses past simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes losing something?
options: I left it on the playground and could not find it.
I left it on the playground and can not find it.
I leave it on the playground and could not find it.
I left it on the playground and couldn't found it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Left' and 'could not find' are both past simple forms.
mcQuestions.hint: Both verbs must be in the past tense.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the best phrase to express strong happiness as a child.
options: I was so happy when she walked through the door.
I am so happy when she walked through the door.
I was so happy when she walks through the door.
I was very happy when she walk through the door.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs should be past simple: 'was' and 'walked'.
mcQuestions.hint: Both verbs in this sentence are in the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrasal verb means something breaks into pieces over time?
options: fell off
fell down
fell over
fell apart
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Fell off' means a part became detached. In the text, one eye detached from the toy.
mcQuestions.hint: One eye became detached from the toy.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence: 'My grandmother ___ it back together with black thread.'
options: sewed
sew
sewn
was sew
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past simple of 'sew'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which adjective means old and damaged from long use?
options: worn out
worn off
wear out
weared out
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Worn out' is an adjective meaning very old and damaged from use.
mcQuestions.hint: The past participle form used as an adjective.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly contrasts the toy's condition with the writer's feelings?
options: The toy became very worn out, but I did not care.
The toy became very worn out, so I did not care.
The toy became very worn out, because I did not care.
The toy became very worn out, although I cared.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'But' shows contrast between two opposite ideas.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best ending sentence to reflect on a childhood memory.
options: It reminds me of a time when everything was simple and safe.
It reminds me of a time when everything is simple and safe.
It reminded me of a time when everything was simple and safe.
It remind me of a time when everything was simple and safe.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple 'reminds' is used because the feeling exists now. The past clause uses 'was'.
mcQuestions.hint: The feeling is present now, but the time described is past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means to hold something tightly with your hands?
options: holding it tightly
holding it hardly
keeping it tightly
grabbing it nearly
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Holding tightly' is the natural collocation for gripping something firmly.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do you think people keep old toys or childhood objects for many years? What does a favourite toy say about a person's childhood?
